name: ExtensionImagingSelection_Focus
title: R5: Related resource that is the focus for the imaging selection (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ImagingSelection.focus` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ImagingSelection.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ImagingSelection)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `ImagingSelection.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ImagingSelection)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Related resource that is the focus for the imaging selection (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
An imaging selection may reference a DICOM resource that itself references other DICOM resources.
       e.g. a presentation state references a set of source images or frames.

element.short: Related resource that is the focus for the imaging selection
element.definition: The actual focus of an observation when it is not the patient of record representing something or someone associated with the patient such as a spouse, parent, fetus, or donor. For example, fetus observations in a mother's record.  The focus of an observation could also be an existing condition,  an intervention, the subject's diet,  another observation of the subject,  or a body structure such as tumor or implanted device.   An example use case would be using the Observation resource to capture whether the mother is trained to change her child's tracheostomy tube. In this example, the child is the patient of record and the mother is the focus.
element.comment:
An imaging selection may reference a DICOM resource that itself references other DICOM resources.
       e.g. a presentation state references a set of source images or frames.
